author: comeonnownocensor
title: Amazing App
body: Great app

author: hgddyrfghg
title: Terrible app
body: I’m in the Yosemite valley. I need a charge. This garbage app only makes me “plan a trip” I don’t need to plan Shht! I need a charge!

author: JohnGalt1717
title: Key support is horrible
body:
Effectively always gets who’s in the drive seat wrong. Moves the seat if you get out of the driver’s seat to whomever is in the passenger seat, doesn’t return your seat to you when you get in on the driver’s door. Basically everything that UWB was supposed to solve is worse, not better. 

Also needs the ability to change AC charging amps directly in the app.

author: TestUser1
title: Charging network buggy
body: The app crashes when I try to find a charger. Keeps saying network error.

author: Michael Santarcangelo
title: apple watch app / key issues
body: the apple watch car key functionality seems to have broken in the latest update. i previously was able to open the apple watch key and use the lock / unlock button, which was useful at home where i have passive entry set to lock only. now when i open the key on apple watch there is no lock/unlock button so i cannot use the watch to unlock my car at home. furthermore it seems like it is not possible to add a complication to the watch face that will simply open the rivian watch app - which would be very useful for quick access to the app on the watch without having to scroll the apps list.

author: JudyPGoods
title: Now slower and buggier
body:
The Rivian app has always been a bit mid. This reached a whole new level of issue since the Rivian “intelligence” rollout. I got a buggy AI assistant I don’t want in exchange for:
- painful 30s-1min wake-ups to unlock the car I am standing next to and just locked 
- even more unreliable multi-factor drive
- constant nags to connect data my car should never need
- a slow and poorly animated “AI” to incorrectly search for Spotify songs

author: Mark Bobster
title: I wish it had CarPlay
body:
The Rivian is a great car. My only big gripe is that without CarPlay it’s like going back in time ten years on the media experience. 

They’ve built their own versions of Maps, Spotify, etc, and none of them are very good. AND they charge you $15/month to use them.

My only other big gripe with the car is the doors don’t open when you walk up to it. You have to touch your phone to the door.

author: TestUser2
title: Cannot log in after update
body: Since the update I can't sign in to my account. Password reset doesn't work.

author: MikeNYIM
title: Fix 3.11.1
body: App shows car not charging on phone Home Screen. App shows car unplugged in app energy.

author: hairypothead
title: Buggy
body: The app is basically useless on Gen1 vehicles, which is an obvious sign of how Rivian will run in the future with forced obsolescence. Proximity is buggy and aggressive - sometimes it responds to proximity, other times it doesn’t. If you have proximity on, you CANNOT use any of the remote functions when you’re not close to the vehicle. If you have proximity turned off, the remote app is hit or miss. I have prompted for a frunk open, and the app sent the request 10 minutes later.

author: Brad Fitzpatrick
title: Widget broke
body: This recent update broke my home screen widget which was the primary way I had used the app. Now I just see a black box.

author: jpkrinoid
title: Latest update broke public charging
body: Latest update broke public charging.  Nothing happens after selecting the charger.  App is completely useless.  Actually do QA before you release and break public charging at Rivian chargers!

author: TestUser3
title: Love this app
body: Best EV charging app out there. Works perfectly every time.

author: Darkavich
title: Bugs in this version
body: The latest version no longer updates on the iPhone lock screen. It shows time to charge and state of charge but never updates.

author: csandman9
title: Pathetic
body: Just when you thought Tesla had the worst customer service on earth, Rivian now takes the prize.

author: Roger Peterson
title: Latest update brings back annoying bug
body:
For several months, the Rivian app finally removed the notification every single time you closed the app “to use your phone as a key, you’ll need the app open”

Well, now that notification is back, every SINGLE time you close the app. 

I know for a fact you don’t need the app open to use your phone as a key! Please fix this Rivian it’s SO annoying.

author: Origreenberg
title: Bad experience
body: Barcode on the charging station cannot be scanned, I need to search for the station on the map, and couldn’t find mine. They place them intentionally near places with bad signal, Yosemite for example, where the whole experience relies on having solid internet connection. Bad user experience

author: ACDaniel777
title: Work restricted
body: AC Transit in Bay Area CA allows employees access to Tesla accounts app on their work phones but restricts employees to access Rivian accounts. What’s up with that?

author: mjhns2
title: Haven’t Taken Delivery, But
body:
As the title says, I haven’t taken delivery yet, but so far I’m a little disappointed with the app coming from a Tesla. With the Tesla app, I could see how much I charged at home over the last 30 days and use that amount to pay toward the electric bill. The Rivian app doesn’t seem to have that option.

Edit: The app should have a way to filter compatible charging stations by my vehicle and adapters I have.

author: vince allan
title: Have a backup plan
body: Sometimes it lets me into the car. Other times I get a ride with friends and that’s cool too.
